title: Strata — an optimizing compiler to WebAssembly
description: A from-scratch optimizing compiler that turns a small C-like language into real WebAssembly bytecode in your browser: SSA, SCCP/GVN/LICM/DCE/inlining, if-conversion to branchless select, and a relooper backend. Now prints floating point the hard way: str(float) is a genuine Dragon4 shortest-round-trip formatter written in the language itself and compiled to wasm, matching a browser's Number.toString byte-for-byte (proven by a 10M-double fuzz), alongside an f64 math library (sqrt/floor/ceil/round/abs/fmin/fmax/copysign). Plus structs (aggregate value types) with nested/recursive layouts and a null handle, a genuine 64-bit long lowering to wasm i64 (BigInt-exact, hex/L literals, long[], str(long)), bit primitives (popcount/clz/ctz/rotl/rotr), do-while/switch, a string standard library with str[] (split/join), a step debugger, and a differential test suite proving the wasm matches a reference interpreter at every opt level (492 checks across -O0..-O3).
